instruction: Create an SFTP-only user named charles with password Ex@mpleP@55w0rd!, home directory /home/test1, shell /usr/sbin/nologin, and primary group sftpusers. Set /home/test1 ownership to root:root with permission 755, then create /home/test1/upload owned by charles:sftpusers with permission 750.

parameters.command: echo {CLIENT_PASSWORD} | sudo -S bash -c 'userdel -r charles >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; groupdel sftpusers >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; rm -rf /home/test1; mkdir -p /home/test1'

result.command:
ok=1; getent group sftpusers >/dev/null 2>&1 || ok=0; getent passwd charles >/dev/null 2>&1 || ok=0; [ "$(getent passwd charles | cut -d: -f6)" = "/home/test1" ] || ok=0; [ "$(getent passwd charles | cut -d: -f7)" = "/usr/sbin/nologin" ] || ok=0; id -nG charles 2>/dev/null | tr ' ' '
' | grep -qx 'sftpusers' || ok=0; [ "$(stat -c '%U:%G %a' /home/test1 2>/dev/null)" = "root:root 755" ] || ok=0; [ "$(stat -c '%U:%G %a' /home/test1/upload 2>/dev/null)" = "charles:sftpusers 750" ] || ok=0; if [ "$ok" = "1" ]; then echo PASS; else echo FAIL; fi
